case.source: Platinum 'Re-pipe — scope of work' chunk + 'Playbook: Whole-House Repipe (PEX/Copper)'
case.description: Whole-house repipe — old galvanized supply pipes throughout, low water pressure and occasional leaks. Repipe the entire house with PEX, patch and paint the walls after.

required.label: New PEX/copper pipes up to code
Patch walls and paint after repipe

lines: Pull plumbing permit
Shut off main water supply, drain the system, and notify occupants of water downtime
Map existing routing - supply origin, fixture branches, water heater connection, hose bibs
Test painted surfaces for lead (if pre-1978) and old pipe insulation/wrap for asbestos before disturbing, per AQMD Rule 1403
Cut access points in walls/ceilings as needed, routing to minimize demo (attic/crawlspace runs prioritized where possible)
Remove existing old galvanized supply pipes throughout the house
Run new PEX supply lines from the main to every fixture in the house: kitchen, bathrooms, laundry, water heater (copper at exposed/exterior runs or where code/client preference requires)
Install strap/retrofit the new pipes
Install foam insulation on all exposed hot water pipes
Connect all fixtures in the house: water heater, hose bibs, angle stops, supply lines, faucets, toilets
Pressure-test the entire new system before closing up any walls
Schedule and pass rough plumbing inspection with lines still exposed
Close up walls and ceilings; patch drywall at all plumbing access points
Reconnect all fixtures (angle stops, supply lines, faucets, toilets)
Cover floors and furniture as needed; prepare all working areas
Open cracks wider and fill up cracks; fix and patch all walls
Sand walls
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint on all patched wall areas (local patch and paint only - will not match 100%, expect up to 85% match)
Paint trim around walls/baseboards as needed at patched areas
Place everything back to pre-existing state
Schedule and pass final inspection
Test every fixture in the house for flow, pressure, and leaks under normal use
Clean up and haul away debris

notes: Assumed drywall (not plaster) wall construction and a standard 2-3 bath single-family home for patch/paint scope - confirm with estimator if different.
Assumed PEX throughout per client's description, with copper reserved only for exposed/exterior runs or where code/HOA requires - confirm client isn't requesting all-copper.

No fixtures (faucets, toilets, valves) are being swapped per the client's description, so CalGreen water-efficiency fixture requirements were not included - flag if scope changes to include fixture replacement.
Paint scope covers patched areas only (spot patch/paint), not a full-room repaint - confirm with client whether full-wall or full-room repaint is expected instead of local patching.

groundedOn.title: Acoustic ceiling removal — scope of work
